paper.title_en: The LHCb RICH detectors: Operations and performance
paper.abstract_en: The LHCb experiment at the LHC employs two Ring-Imaging Cherenkov detectors to identify charged hadrons in the momentum range 3–<math altimg="si1.svg" display="inline" id="d1e163"><mrow><mn>100</mn><mspace class="nbsp" width="0.33em"/><mi mathvariant="normal">GeV</mi><mo>/</mo><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>. During Long Shutdown 2, the system was upgraded to operate at a five-fold higher instantaneous luminosity with a <math altimg="si2.svg" display="inline" id="d1e176"><mrow><mn>40</mn><mspace class="nbsp" width="0.33em"/><mi mathvariant="normal">MHz</mi></mrow></math> continuous readout as part of the LHCb Upgrade I programme. The year 2024 marked the first physics production at design conditions. This contribution summarises the operational experience of the RICH system, online monitoring and calibration tools, and the first hadron-identification performance results obtained during Run 3. The upgraded detectors demonstrate robust stability, effective automation, and an improved PID performance with respect to Run 1 and 2.
